name: EZ Pawn Corp
description_short: EZ Pawn Corp Long Island City, New York — Family-owned pawnbroker with 17 NYC locations since 1996, offering collateral-based loans, item appraisals, an...
description_long:
EZ Pawn Corp is a third-generation family pawnbroking business established in 1996 by David Kaminsky, following his father Martin's founding of Gem Pawnbrokers in Brooklyn in 1947. The company operates as a traditional pawnshop, providing short-term collateral-based loans to customers in financial need. With roots tracing back to post-World War II New York, the business has grown into one of the fastest-expanding pawnbroking chains in NYC, now operating 17 conveniently located storefronts across the city.

EZ Pawn Corp offers three primary services: pawning items for immediate cash loans, selling items outright, and buying merchandise from customers. Their loan process involves bringing items of value to a location, having knowledgeable loan officers provide appraisals, and receiving loan agreements with cash in hand the same day. The company charges 4% per month in loan rates and fees, with special provisions for active-duty military members and their dependents receiving 36% APR in compliance with the Military Lending Act. They also provide a free loan calculator on their website for customer estimates.

The company distinguishes itself through emphasis on family heritage, community involvement, and customer service reputation. EZ Pawn Corp partners with local charities including the Kiss Cares Coat Drive and NYC AIDS Walk. The business has received media coverage from CBS 2 New York and The New York Times, with company leadership highlighting their role as providers of short-term "bridge" loans to the community. Customer testimonials consistently praise their professional operations, honest dealings, friendly staff, and fair pricing for both loans and item purchases.

As a pawnshop, EZ Pawn Corp serves customers needing immediate cash against collateral, but borrowers should understand that 4% monthly rates (48% APR) are legally permissible for pawnbrokers but exceed rates available through traditional lenders. The business model requires surrendering physical possessions, with redemption subject to loan agreement terms. While the company maintains strong customer reviews and generational credibility, customers must evaluate whether collateral-based borrowing aligns with their financial situation compared to alternative lending options.
pros: Established 1996 with three generations of family expertise and reputation in pawnbroking
17 conveniently located stores throughout New York City for accessibility
Immediate same-day cash payment with no waiting period for loan approval
Free online loan calculator providing transparent cost estimates before visiting
Active military and dependents receive 36% APR under Military Lending Act compliance
Consistent 5-star customer testimonials praising professional service and fair pricing
Community involvement through partnerships with local charities and giving-back initiatives
Knowledgeable appraisers for accurate item valuations and transparent loan agreements
cons: 4% monthly interest rate (48% APR) is legally high for pawnbrokers, though lawful in NY
Requires surrendering physical possessions as collateral with redemption contingent on repayment
Additional charges mentioned (storage fees, handling fees, late fees) beyond stated 4% monthly rate
Limited service area restricted to New York City, no online or mail-in pawn options available
Loan terms appear short-term focused, not suitable for customers needing extended repayment periods
best_for: NYC residents needing immediate cash urgently who own valuable items (jewelry, electronics, watches)
Individuals seeking quick bridge loans between paychecks without credit checks or approval delays
People looking to sell used items directly or on consignment at local pawn locations
Active-duty military members and dependents accessing the 36% APR rate alternative
diagnosis: EZ Pawn Corp is best for NYC residents in immediate financial emergencies who own valuable collateral and can access one of their 17 locations. The primary caveat is that 4% monthly interest (48% APR) is substantially higher than credit cards, personal loans, or bank lines of credit, making this a borrowing option of last resort rather than an efficient financing choice.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans with same-day cash payment
Item appraisals by knowledgeable loan officers
Buying items outright from customers for cash
Selling used merchandise from retail inventory
Free online loan calculator for cost estimates
Military Lending Act compliant 36% APR loans for active duty and dependents
Storage and item holding during loan periods
14+ days loan redemption window with interest and fees
